mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly reducing present values of future corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and moderate inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without overheating, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in > 50% of occurrences or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which boosts short-term market sentiment and yields positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict direction with accuracy > 52% during recovery phases or loses predictive power when volatility exceeds 20%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp year-over-year contraction in retail sales indicates a sudden collapse in consumer demand, forcing earnings downgrades and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Market absorbs retail sales declines without negative returns in > 65% of cases or signal decays after 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy creates a stagflationary environment that compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate during high-inflation rate-hike periods or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no correlation with forward returns or produces negative returns in > 55% of months with positive payroll changes.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly acceleration in industrial production signals immediate supply-side momentum that outweighs labor market concerns, driving rapid positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 50% when unemployment is above 5.5% or returns revert to mean within 3 trading days.
